task: Diagnose why snipes aren't firing and scout trades are low — is the system broken or is it market conditions?
opus_correction: Systematic 4-pipeline diagnostic: scout (767 scans, 5 alerts, fan-contracting filter), snipe (4 triggers, all pair_cooldown blocked after USD_JPY loss), validator (6 verdicts breakdown), trading cycles (12 normal). Root cause: pair_cooldown + market conditions, not system failure.
opus_reasoning: User conflated multiple hypotheses (open trades blocking, broken system, market). Needed to test each independently by querying scout scan counts, snipe gate logs, and validator verdicts separately.

task: Fix position_guardian.py so scout trades get same grace period as manual trades
opus_correction: 4-location fix in position_guardian.py: (1) score_threat grace flag includes source=scout, (2) SL ratcheting uses 15% rate for scout, (3) DB init check recognizes scout source, (4) log label updated. Verified EUR/GBP #3931 would survive under new rules.
opus_reasoning: The is_manual flag was checked in multiple codepaths — fixing only score_threat would leave SL ratcheting and DB init using the old logic. All 4 locations needed consistent treatment of scout=manual equivalence.

task: Connection doctor repair — triage 11 pending items and fix database issues
opus_correction: Triage into 3 categories: fixable (2 DBs with transient WAL degradation), skip-by-design (3 on-demand servers, 5 old backup copies, 1 stale readme). Discovered connection_doctor.db itself broken with stale journal — rebuilt from schema. Identified sandbox sqlite limitation requiring copy-to-/tmp workaround.
opus_reasoning: Bulk-resolving all items would mask real issues. Each item needed individual assessment against architecture design (MLX idle-by-design) and active vs backup status. The meta-issue (doctor DB itself broken) was only discovered by attempting writes.
